claim: The AGUI (Agent-User Interaction) protocol defines 16 standard events optimized for agent-user communication and is transport-agnostic
actionable: Use AGUI servers and clients to enable standardized agent-UI interactions across different transport protocols
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Tyler Slaton: Build User Facing Agentic Applications

claim: Agentic applications break the request-response paradigm because they are long-running processes requiring streaming for good UX
actionable: Design agentic UIs expecting streaming responses, not traditional request-response patterns
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Tyler Slaton: Build User Facing Agentic Applications

claim: Static generative UI (mapping generated data to existing components) provides deep control but creates tight coupling between backend and frontend
actionable: Use static generative UI for common, well-defined UI patterns; avoid it when your agent needs flexibility
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Tyler Slaton: Build User Facing Agentic Applications

claim: Open-ended generative UI (HTML/iframe rendering) allows agent teams to build UI without coupling but may render unpredictably and creates styling difficulties
actionable: Use open-ended UI when agents need freedom but establish style guidelines and test rendering across clients
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Tyler Slaton: Build User Facing Agentic Applications

claim: Declarative UI (component-based schemas like JSON UI) balances flexibility and consistency but is constrained to predefined components
actionable: Design declarative UI specs that cover your common use cases while allowing agent flexibility within the schema
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Tyler Slaton: Build User Facing Agentic Applications

claim: Agent state updates should be bidirectional - agents can both read state from the UI and receive state updates from users
actionable: Implement bidirectional state synchronization in agentic applications to enable collaborative editing between agents and users
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Tyler Slaton: Build User Facing Agentic Applications

claim: Voice modality for agents is becoming critical in 2026, enabling real-time agent interactions with generative UI feedback without waiting for text responses
actionable: Design agents to support voice input and streaming voice output with generative UI responses
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Tyler Slaton: Build User Facing Agentic Applications
